content:
The **sigmoid** (logistic) curve predates deep learning. **Verhulst** (1838–1845) introduced the logistic function to model population growth with a natural carrying capacity — an S-shaped saturation to an upper bound, the same mathematical shape reused for probability squashing.

**Pearson** and early 20th-century statisticians developed **logit** models for binary outcomes. **Berkson** (1944) applied the logistic transform to biostatistics, linking linear predictors to bounded probabilities.

**McCulloch and Pitts** (1943) framed artificial neurons with threshold logic; by the **1950s–60s**, **Rosenblatt's perceptron** and early **multilayer networks** experimented with differentiable squashing functions. **Werbos** (1974) and **Rumelhart, Hinton, and Williams** (1986) popularized **backpropagation** through sigmoid hidden units — making the activation the workhorse of the first neural-network renaissance.

The **vanishing-gradient** limitation of deep sigmoid stacks became a central motivation for **LSTM** gating (Hochreiter & Schmidhuber, 1997 — sigmoid gates with separate cell paths) and later for **ReLU** hidden activations (Nair & Hinton, 2010), which largely displaced sigmoid in feedforward depth while leaving it on outputs and gates.

timeline.label: Logistic curve (1838–1845)
timeline.description: Verhulst models saturating growth with the logistic function — early appearance of the S-curve later called sigmoid.

timeline.label: Logit statistics (1940s)
timeline.description: Berkson and contemporaries use the logistic transform to link linear predictors to probabilities in (0, 1).

timeline.label: Backpropagation era (1986)
timeline.description: Rumelhart, Hinton, and Williams train multilayer nets with sigmoid activations via backprop — deep learning's first widespread success.

timeline.label: LSTM gates (1997)
timeline.description: Sigmoid retained for forget/input/output gates while tanh handles cell content — bounded gates without sigmoid in every hidden layer.

timeline.label: ReLU replaces deep sigmoid (2010s)
timeline.description: ReLU becomes the default hidden activation in deep feedforward CNNs and MLPs; sigmoid remains on binary outputs and gates.
